payload.description: Primary connection between Downtown Brooklyn, Fort Greene, Clinton Hill, Bed-Stuy, Bushwick, and Ridgewood. Local and Limited-stop service – Limited runs weekdays 6:30 AM – 7:30 PM. Arrives every 5-10 minutes during peak hours. Transfers to every subway line in Brooklyn.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Primary connection between Downtown Brooklyn, Fort Greene, Clinton Hill, Bed-Stuy, Bushwick, and Ridgewood • 18,000+ average daily riders • Local and Limited-stop service – Limited runs weekdays 6:30 AM – 7:30 PM • Arrives every 5-10 minutes during peak hours • Transfers to every subway line in Brooklyn • Slow speeds, around 4-8 MPH on Dekalb-Lafayette corridor

tool_args.label: Dekalb-Lafayette Aves Bus and Safety Improvements

payload.project_name: Dekalb-Lafayette Aves Bus and Safety Improvements
payload.description: NYC DOT and MTA study to improve bus service along the B38 route on Dekalb and Lafayette Avenues, including bus priority and safety improvements. Presented to Brooklyn Community Board 3 in December 2024.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Dekalb-Lafayette Aves Bus and Safety Improvements
NYC DOT and MTA seeks to improve bus service along the B38 route

payload.description: Dekalb Avenue corridor from Flatbush Av to Bedford Av, studied for bus and safety improvements. Includes segments: Malcolm X Blvd to Cumberland St (40' width), Cumberland St to Flatbush Av (42' width). Bus speeds as low as 4 mph.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Bus speeds are as low as 4 mph on this critical corridor connecting Brooklyn and Queens
Dekalb Av: Malcolm X Blvd to Cumberland St
Dekalb Av: Cumberland St to Flatbush Av

issues: evidence_refs[0].source_quote is not present in the cited block

payload.description: Lafayette Avenue corridor studied for bus and safety improvements. Includes segments: Fulton St to South Elliott Pl (33' width), South Elliott Pl to Classon Av (40' width), Classon Av to Marcy Av (40' width, no bike lane), Marcy Av to Patchen Av (33' width). Vision Zero Priority Corridor.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Lafayette Av is a Vision Zero Priority Corridor
Lafayette Av: Fulton St to South Elliott Pl
Lafayette Av: South Elliott Pl to Classon Av
Lafayette Av: Classon Av to Marcy Av
Lafayette Av: Marcy Av to Patchen Av

payload.description: Dekalb Avenue corridor studied for bus and safety improvements. Includes segments: Malcolm X Blvd to Cumberland St (40' width), Cumberland St to Flatbush Av (42' width). Bus speeds as low as 4 mph.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Dekalb Av: Malcolm X Blvd to Cumberland St
Dekalb Av: Cumberland St to Flatbush Av

payload.location_text: Portions of DeKalb and Lafayette Aves
payload.description: Temporary bus lanes installed during the Summer 2024 G Train shutdown for shuttles along portions of DeKalb and Lafayette Aves. Improved B38 service speeds up to 21% on DeKalb Av and up to 5% on Lafayette Av.

payload.raw_value_text: 215,000 people live within the study area

payload.raw_value_text: bus rider median annual income is lower than drivers ($30,000 vs. $47,000)

payload.claim_text: Protected bike lanes: 34% reduction in risk of injury. On the highest-risk streets, cycling risk or injury is reduced by over 60%.

evidence_refs.source_quote: 34% reduction in risk of injury • On the highest-risk streets, cycling risk or injury is reduced by over 60%

payload.claim_text: Standard bike lanes: 32% reduction in risk of injury. Improved safety on all study projects.

evidence_refs.source_quote: 32% reduction in risk of injury • Improved safety on all study projects

payload.claim_text: Shared lanes: 18% reduction in risk of injury across all projects. Limited use (wayfinding, as part of bike blvds, or on very narrow/low volume streets).

evidence_refs.source_quote: 18% reduction in risk of injury across all projects • Limited use (wayfinding, as part of bike blvds, or on very narrow/low volume streets)
